summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: State interchange fee laws progress
articles.description: Legislatures in Colorado and Delaware advanced bills that would curtail interchange fees last week, as a federal appellate court mulls the issue.

articles.title: Mastercard to buy BVNK for $1.8B
articles.description: “Adding on-chain rails to our network will support speed and programmability for virtually every type of transaction,” a Mastercard executive said.

articles.title: EWS pairs with Citi to offer Paze
articles.description: The collaboration marks the first time the financial technology company has worked with a non-owner bank on the digital wallet.

articles.title: FDIC to make stablecoin move
articles.description: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. plans to propose that pass-through insurance not be available for stablecoin deposits.

articles.title: CFPB again told it must request funds from Fed
articles.description: In the second court ruling rejecting the bureau’s position on requesting Fed funds, a judge in California called agency Acting Director Russ Vought’s plan a “transparent display of partisanship.”

articles.title: Fintechs push 36% state rate caps
articles.description: While there is a 36% interest rate standard for lending in the U.S., it’s not codified for all consumers. Now, the effort to set one has moved to the states.

articles.title: Payments firms diverge on checks
articles.description: Payment companies offered different ways to move past paper checks in comments to the Federal Reserve regarding plans to shift to digital alternatives.

articles.title: Robinhood takes on premium card issuers
articles.description: The fintech’s new $695 credit card aims to lure young, wealthy people uninterested in hoarding points or using a card for airport lounge access.
